prediction.attackType: Server Software Component: Web Shell (MITRE T1505.003)
prediction.summary: An attacker from IP 10.20.8.77 successfully uploaded a web shell named 'shell.php' to the '/uploads/' directory of a web server. Following the upload, the attacker used the web shell to execute commands such as 'whoami' and 'cat /etc/passwd', and attempted to download a second-stage payload. Multiple security systems, including WAF, IDS, and SIEM, detected and alerted on this malicious activity, confirming a critical web shell compromise.

timeline.event: WAF alerted on suspicious PHP upload to webroot /uploads/shell.php from 10.20.8.77.
Client 10.20.8.77 attempted to download 'stage2' from http://evil.tld/ via /uploads/shell.php.
IDS detected web shell activity: repeated POST to .php with cmd parameter.
SIEM identified MITRE T1505.003 Server Software Component: Web Shell.
ModSecurity blocked a SQLi probe from 10.20.8.77 targeting /uploads/shell.php.
SIEM issued CRITICAL alert for webshell /uploads/shell.php on host webserver01 from source 10.20.8.77.

recommendations: Immediately isolate the compromised web server (webserver01) to prevent further attacker activity and lateral movement.
Remove the uploaded web shell (/uploads/shell.php) and any other potentially malicious files (e.g., 'stage2' if downloaded) from the server.
Conduct a full forensic analysis of the web server to determine the initial compromise vector, the extent of attacker access, and any data exfiltration or system modifications.
Review web server access logs and other system logs for any additional suspicious activity from 10.20.8.77 or other related IPs.
Update WAF/IDS rules to proactively block similar web shell uploads, command execution patterns, and known malicious IPs/domains.
Implement application-level controls to restrict file uploads to specific types and locations, and enforce least privilege principles for web application directories.
